title: Percolation Critical Probability of Aperiodic Smith Hat tile(1, $\sqrt3$)
abstract: The Smith Hat tile is the first known aperiodic monotile, having been discovered in 2023. The simple structure, constructed using only 8 kites, is unique and well motivated for analysis within percolation theory. The primary goal of this paper is to discover the critical threshold $p_c$ in both site and bond Bernoulli structures using Monte Carlo simulation for the Smith hat tile(1,$\sqrt3$). Our findings are site and bond values of $p_c^s = 0.822725 \pm 0.000044$ and $p_c^b = 0.798161 \pm 0.000044$ for edge percolation and $0.544247 \pm 0.000101$ for site percolation on the dual graph.
summary: This paper provides the first numerical benchmarks for percolation thresholds on the recently discovered Smith Hat aperiodic monotile. By using Monte Carlo simulations and finite-size scaling, the authors calculate specific critical probabilities for both site and bond percolation. The results show that the unique geometry of the hat tile leads to higher percolation thresholds compared to standard periodic lattices. This work contributes to the understanding of how aperiodicity affects phase transitions and global connectivity in tiling systems.
main_problem: Determining the percolation critical thresholds (pc) for the Smith Hat tile, the first discovered aperiodic monotile, and investigating how its non-translational geometry influences connectivity.
main_result: The study establishes site and bond percolation thresholds for edge percolation (ps = 0.822725, pb = 0.798161) and site percolation on the dual graph (ps = 0.544247), finding these values are higher than common periodic lattices.
method: Monte Carlo simulations using a Union-Find approach and Finite-Size Scaling (FSS) with the L^-1/nu scaling relation to extrapolate results to the infinite system limit.
model_or_system: The Smith Hat tiling, an aoperiodic tiling of the plane using a single 13-sided prototile, analyzed via Bernoulli site and bond percolation models on both the tiling edges and the dual graph.
key_observables: Critical probabilities (pc) for site and bond percolation, and the critical exponent (nu).
important_parameters: System size L (ranging from 10 to 400), number of independent trials (T = 1000), and the assumed critical exponent nu = 4/3.
main_assumptions: Assumes the Smith Hat tile is percolation-isotropic and that the critical exponent nu follows the standard 2D percolation universality class (4/3).
figures_summary: The figures illustrate the fundamental hat tile, metatile patterns (H, T, P, F), the construction of the sampling frame, and plots showing the convergence of percolation thresholds and confidence intervals.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the Smith Hat tile, defines the percolation models (site/bond on edges and dual graphs), details the Monte Carlo and Finite-Size Scaling methodology, presents numerical results and validation against known lattices, and discusses limitations and implications.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
